front: What is the role of pH in peptide stability?
back: pH affects peptide stability through: 1) Acid/base-catalyzed hydrolysis (Asp-Pro bonds, Asn deamidation), 2) Aggregation (charge-dependent), 3) Conformational stability, 4) Solubility. Most peptides are stable at pH 4-7. Storage at 2-8°C in buffered solutions is standard.
